title: Starting and Stopping the Sentinel RMS License Manager on Unix and Linux
breadcrumbs: Installing the Sentinel RMS License Manager on Unix and Linux
Starting and Stopping the Sentinel RMS License Manager on Unix and Linux
text: Starting and Stopping the Sentinel RMS License Manager on Unix and Linux To start or stop the RMS License Manager, use the following commands. On Solaris: /etc/init.d/rms start /etc/init.d/rms stop On Linux, if your system uses systemd : systemctl start rms.target systemctl stop rms.target On AIX: InstallationDirectory /rms.sh start InstallationDirectory /rms.sh stop Note: For cloud-served licenses, these commands start and stop both the RMS License Manager and the Cloud Plug-in at the same time and in the correct order. When starting the RMS License Manager, the following output displays: The RMS license server is running. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
